title: Creamy Indian Paneer Stir-Fry
description: A lazy Sunday savory dish featuring creamy paneer and ready-to-roll shortcrust pastry for a whole30-friendly twist on traditional Rajasthani cuisine.
instructions: If using shortcrust pastry, preheat the oven and have it ready at this temperature. If not using, skip this step.
Heat a pan over medium heat with a tablespoon of oil. Add finely chopped onions and sauté until translucent.
Add minced garlic and cook for another minute or two until fragrant. Stir in tomato puree, makhani masala powder (or garam masala), salt, and water/vegetable broth.
Bring the mixture to a simmer, then reduce heat to low and let it thicken slightly. Simmer gently for about 5-7 minutes.
Fold in cooked paneer cubes with the sauce. Add curry leaves if using and mix well.

If you're not serving immediately, transfer the pan to your oven-safe dish and bake until the paneer is golden brown on top (about 10-12 minutes). If baking, place the dish directly under the preheated oven for best results.
